paragraphs.text: Dear European Commission, firstly, thank you for your time and effort to consider our protection when it comes to video games. I understand gaming is not a topic everyone is fond of (especially concerned parents, in my personal experience). But one thing I will say is that games have changed the way the younger generation - my generation - think and feel. They are an escape when feeling down, a social event for friends, and can be great for enhancing your skills, like hand-eye coordination and reflexes. Games are the cornerstone of any young person's life now, and we cannot simply escape this fact, just like any other fast-moving technology, just like the Internet. Video games are a baby in the grand scheme of things, so we have to put in place the right laws to protect the future generation. So please, think about how we should protect our video games for the future generation. Remember that we are not here forever, and eventually our kids will outlive us - so think about the kind of world we want to leave behind for them - a right and just world where games can be enjoyed without any predatory, anti-consumer practices in place. We just want to enjoy games and have fun, that's really all it comes down to. Thank you for your time and consideration
